shorts.content: Leadership doesn't start in the boardroom—it starts in your own living room. If you can't manage yourself, how can you expect to manage others? Self-management is the foundation of great leadership.
shorts.title: Where Real Leadership Actually Begins

shorts.hook: You have to know who you don't want to be
shorts.content: You have to know who you don't want to be just as much as you know who you are. Think about the worst boss you've ever had. What did they do that made you cringe? Now make sure you never do those things.
shorts.title: The Leadership Rule Nobody Talks About

shorts.content: Mothers naturally develop management skills through daily parenting that translate directly to workplace leadership. You're already coordinating schedules, managing resources, and leading a team at home.
shorts.title: Why Moms Make The Best Managers

shorts.content: Tactical missions and small daily practices build leadership confidence more effectively than waiting for formal titles. Start leading where you are right now with what you have.
shorts.title: How to Lead Without the Title

titleOptions.curiosityGap: What Happens When Leaders Skip This Foundation Step?
titleOptions.boldStatement: Self-Management is the Foundation of Great Leadership
titleOptions.numberBased: 5 Self-Management Skills Every Leader Must Master

description:
Think you're ready to lead others? Former corporate executive Lynda Harvey reveals the one skill you must master first—and it's not what you think.
Discover why the best leaders start by managing themselves and how your invisible work is already building leadership skills.

⏰ TIMESTAMPS:
0:00 Introduction
3:15 Why self-management comes first
8:42 How motherhood builds management skills
15:20 Knowing who you don't want to be
22:10 Building leadership confidence without a title
28:45 Emotional intelligence in modern leadership
35:30 Creating opportunities through personal branding

🔑 KEY TAKEAWAYS:
• Self-management is the foundational skill for effective leadership
• Mothers develop natural management skills through parenting
• Define who you don't want to be as a leader
• Build confidence through small daily leadership practices
• Emotional intelligence is essential for modern workplace leadership

🎙️ CONNECT WITH GUEST:
📚 Follow Lynda Harvey → Instagram @lynda.leads (https://www.instagram.com/lynda.leads)

📖 FULL SHOW NOTES: https://themanageher.com/blog/how-to-manage-yourself-before-you-manage-others
📊 TAKE THE QUIZ: https://themanageher.com/blog/how-to-manage-yourself-before-you-manage-others

🔔 SUBSCRIBE to The Manage Her® Podcast for weekly leadership insights for women!

#Leadership #SelfManagement #WomenInLeadership #ManagementTips #CareerGrowth #WorkingMoms #LeadershipSkills #EmotionalIntelligence #PersonalDevelopment #WomenLeaders

communityPosts.content:
What's the biggest challenge you face when trying to lead others?

A) Managing my own emotions first
B) Getting people to take me seriously
C) Balancing authority with empathy
D) Building confidence without a formal title

Just dropped a new episode with Lynda Harvey about why self-management is the foundation of great leadership. The answer might surprise you! 🎙️

"Leadership doesn't start in the boardroom—it starts in your own living room." - Lynda Harvey

This quote from our latest podcast episode hit me hard. 💭

We spend so much time trying to prove we're ready to lead others, but how often do we focus on leading ourselves first?

If you can't manage your own reactions, emotions, and daily practices, how can you expect to guide a team through challenges?

What's one way you practice self-management as a leader? Drop it in the comments! 👇
